title: baccharis racemosa var. typica
intro: Baccharis racemosa var. typica, also known as Coyote Mint or Desert Coyote Mint, is a shrub-like plant native to the Southwestern United States and Northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Baccharis racemosa var. typica include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. Baccharis racemosa var. typica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
